content_markdown:
The Earth is distance $R_{\text{M}}$ from the Moon and distance $R_{\text{S}}$ from the Sun. The ratio

$$\frac{\text{gravitational field strength at the Earth due to the Moon}}{\text{gravitational field strength at the Earth due to the Sun}}$$

is proportional to which of the following?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: The gravitational field strength $g$ at a distance $r$ from a mass $M$ is given by $g = \frac{GM}{r^2}$. Therefore, $g$ is inversely proportional to the square of the distance ($g \propto \frac{1}{r^2}$). For the Moon, $g_{\text{M}} \propto \frac{1}{R_{\text{M}}^2}$, and for the Sun, $g_{\text{S}} \propto \frac{1}{R_{\text{S}}^2}$. The ratio $\frac{g_{\text{M}}}{g_{\text{S}}}$ is proportional to $\frac{1/R_{\text{M}}^2}{1/R_{\text{S}}^2}$, which simplifies to $\frac{R_{\text{S}}^2}{R_{\text{M}}^2}$.
